quoteFirst: This decidedly gave the boat upwards of five miles an hour, the tide sometimes against us, neither the sails nor any implement but the steam used. The next morning we left Albany at
quoteLast: and arrived at Clermont in nine hours precisely, which gave us five miles an hour. The current on returning was stronger than when going up.
title: Robert Fulton
author: Sutcliffe, Alice Crary

title: A Voyage in the 'Sunbeam', Our Home on the Ocean for Eleven Months
author: Brassey, Annie, 1839-1887

quoteFirst: 23d--Marched up the Rosebud, passing many old Indian camps, and following a very large lodge-pole trail, but not fresh, making 33 miles. 24th--The march was continued up the Rosebud, the trail and signs freshening with every mile until we had made 28 miles, and we then encamped and waited for information from the scouts. At
quoteLast: , Custer called the officers together, and informed us that beyond a doubt the village was in the valley of the Little Big Horn, and that to reach it, it was necessary to cross the divide between the Rosebud and Little Big Horn, and it would be impossible to do so in the daytime without discovering our march to the Indians; that we would prepare to move at 11 P.M. This was done, the line of march turning from the Rosebud to the right, up one of its branches, which headed near the summit of the divide.
title:
Eleven Years in the Rocky Mountains and Life on the Frontier
Also a History of the Sioux War, and a Life of Gen. George A. Custer with Full Account of His Last Battle
author: Victor, Frances Fuller, 1826-1902

quoteLast: , so it would not be long before he came.
title: The Corsican Brothers
author: Dumas, Alexandre, 1802-1870; Frith, Henry, 1840- [Translator]

quoteFirst: Our masts, yards, sails, and rigging, very much damaged; the mizen-mast, dangerously wounded, struck the mizen yard, and sent topgallant-masts and yards down upon deck, unbent the mizen topsail, a spritsail, topsail, and a jib for a mizen. At
quoteLast: saw the Prince George to leeward without a fore-mast. Employed fishing the fore and mizen topsail yards, and fitting the rigging, and shifting powder from forward to aft, and cleared the decks up ready for action.
title: Memoirs and Correspondence of Admiral Lord de Saumarez. Vol II
author: Ross, John, Sir, 1777-1856

quoteFirst: In the afternoon she had some cocoa, which she has not brought up. Towards the evening she felt oppressed, which, however, subsided after a warm bath, with cold douche. At
quoteLast: another injection of a quarter of a grain of morphia had to be given, on account of symptoms of a collapse showing themselves. Only three-quarters of an hour afterwards the good effect was visible.
title: Drugs that enslave
author: Kane, H. H. (Harry Hubbell), 1854-1906

quoteFirst: "You will carry, each, a crash towel, a sponge, and soap," she announced to Katy, "and will be in the entry, at the foot of the stairs, at
quoteLast: precisely.  Failures in punctuality will be punished by a mark."  Miss Jane always delivered her words like a machine, and closed her mouth with a snap at the end of the sentence.
title: What Katy Did at School
author: Coolidge, Susan, 1835-1905

quoteFirst: The after-shocks in 1883 were much more numerous than in 1881. Between
quoteLast: on July 28th and noon on August 3rd, twenty-one slight shocks were recorded at Casamicciola. At 2.15 P.M. on August 3rd, a violent shock occurred that caused further damage at Forio, and even at places so far from the epicentre as Fiaiano, Barano, and Fontana, and increased the displacements of the landslips on Epomeo.
title: A Study of Recent Earthquakes
author: Davison, Charles, 1858-1940

title: A Voyage in the 'Sunbeam', Our Home on the Ocean for Eleven Months
